metadata.title: Generate-Evaluate-Select with Claude: Three Responses, One Real Decision

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: One Claude response is a default. Three responses are a choice. The selection judgment is the part you can never delegate.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: One Claude response is a default. Three responses are a choice. The selection judgment is the part you can never delegate.

beats.narration_text: Generate-evaluate-select is not a workaround for Claude being inconsistent — it is a technique for converting a default into a deliberate choice. The same prompt, three separate sessions, produces three pedagogically different responses. The selection and documented reasoning are the supervisory work. The comparison is what makes the selection defensible.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Generate-evaluate-select is not a workaround for Claude being inconsistent — it is a technique for converting a default into a deliberate choice. The same promp

beats.narration_text: Generate a first-draft feedback paragraph: 80 words, second person, two rubric criteria, no numeric grade. Run three times.

props.command: claude "Generate a first-draft feedback paragraph (~80 words, second person) for a student submission missing the Methodology section and with functions without docstrings. No numeric grade. Reference at least two specific rubric criteria. Plain markdown." # run 3 times

beats.narration_text: Build a comparison script: reads three response files, formats side-by-side table — word count, rubric criteria referenced, framing, equity flags.

props.code:
import re, sys
from pathlib import Path

def analyze(text):
    words = len(text.split())
    criteria = len(re.findall(
        r'(Methodology|docstring|rubric|criterion)',
        text, re.I))
    framing = ('strength' if re.search(
        r'(strong|effective|well)', text, re.I)
        else 'deficit')
    equity_flag = bool(re.search(
        r'(unconventional|unusual|non-standard)',
        text, re.I))
    return words, criteria, framing, equity_flag

for i, f in enumerate(['A','B','C']):
    t = Path(f'response_{f}.md').read_text()
    w, c, fr, eq = analyze(t)
    print(f'Response {f}: {w}w | {c} criteria | '
          f'{fr}-first | equity_flag={eq}')

beats.narration_text: Three candidates: Response A deficit-first, 2 criteria, no equity flags. Response B strength-first, 3 criteria, no flags. Response C deficit-first, 2 criteria, one equity flag — flagged 'unconventional naming.' Selection: Response B. Reasoning logged: strength-first framing, 3 criteria, no equity issues.

beats.narration_text: Generate a fourth response explicitly leading with a strength. Compare to all three.

props.command: claude "Generate a 4th feedback paragraph for the same submission. REQUIRED: lead with a strength before addressing gaps. Same rubric criteria, ~80 words, second person, no grade."

beats.narration_text: Response D strength-first, 2 criteria, no equity flags: similar to Response B but one fewer rubric criterion referenced. Selection still Response B — forced framing did not improve the output. When all three original candidates share a framing problem, the problem is in the specification.

beats.narration_text: Generate-evaluate-select is a dangerous-middle detector: when all three candidates share the same problematic pattern, the pattern is in the specification. The selection judgment — which candidate, and why — is the supervisory work the chapter names Interpretive Judgment. The documented rationale is what makes the selection auditable.
